question: Since Camunda 8.8.20, which user task implementation change is supported by a process instance migration mapping instruction?

options.text: Camunda user task → job-worker user task only.
Both job-worker → Camunda user task and Camunda user task → job-worker user task.
Job-worker user task → Camunda user task only.
No user task implementation change is supported by migration.

a.text: The reverse direction is not supported. Migration cannot change a Camunda user task back into a job-worker user task.

b.text: Only one direction is allowed. Bidirectional user task implementation migration is not supported.

c.text: Since 8.8.20, mapping instructions can change the user task implementation from a job-worker user task to a Camunda user task, but not vice versa. Before 8.8.20, no user task implementation migration was possible at all.

d.text: Before 8.8.20 this was true, but since 8.8.20 job-worker → Camunda user task migration is explicitly supported.

explanation: Per the migration limitations, since Camunda 8.8.20 a mapping instruction can change a user task implementation from a job-worker user task to a Camunda user task, but the reverse direction is not allowed. In versions before 8.8.20 no user task implementation migration was possible.
